_comment: Hand-curated overrides for tip sections — used when the catalog body is too dense to render cleanly. Keyed by tip id. Re-runs of parse-catalog.ts merge these in.

operational-deployment-by-archetype.title: Price practically, list options meaningfully
operational-deployment-by-archetype.body:
**Anchor dishes**
Anchor dishes fund the operation. They're easy to make, low-cost for the kitchen, and the staples your guests come back for. Don't overcharge — keep them accessible. Volume + repeat visits is the play, and a generous portion at a fair price builds the kind of word-of-mouth a marketing budget can't buy.

Lead the menu with these. Hide them at the bottom of a category and you starve the dish that funds everything else.
- **Dal makhani**, **misir wot**, **injera**, **chana masala**, **aloo gobi**, **mujadara**, **hummus**, **cuban black beans**, **mole poblano**, **gazpacho**, **gigantes plaki**, **chili**, **tortilla soup**. Under $1.50 cost, hold 3+ days, plate in <5 min.

**Popular mains**
Between anchors and showcases sits the bread-and-butter — $14–22 plates that fill most tickets. Build these around in-house seitan, tempeh, jackfruit, and king-oyster mushrooms; the value-tier badges (4–8× return) live here. Aim for 60–70% of menu items in this band — that's where service-night profitability actually lives.
- **Mac & cheese**, **pad thai**, **bánh mì**, **falafel**, **bibimbap**, **tofu tikka masala**, **chana masala**, **kung pao tofu**.

**Showcase plates (optional, for fine dining)**
This is really for fine-dining restaurants and businesses willing to invest heavily in the atmosphere, decor, and specialty of their venture. The Kinder World Guide library has a small set of these — **faux gras terrine**, **beef wellington**, **bistecca alla fiorentina**, **bourguignon**, **peking duck** — when you want them.

foodservice-sourcing-summary:
**Chunk Foods** — Sysco, Chefs' Warehouse, or Webstaurant Store. 18-month frozen shelf life is the unlock.

**Juicy Marbles** — direct via juicymarbles.com Food Service, or Webstaurant Store (4 oz × 20/case).

**Beyond & Impossible** — Sysco, US Foods, GFS, Restaurant Depot, Webstaurant. ~$7/lb wholesale floor on ground SKUs. Backbone of any ground-protein rebuild.
menu-engineering-tips-for-vegan-restaura:
1. **Anchor the menu with mid-tier mains.** $14–22 plates fill most tickets — the bread-and-butter of service. Build around in-house seitan, tempeh, jackfruit, and king-oyster mushrooms; that's where service-night profit actually lives.
2. **Bundle bulk-prep into combo platters.** Ethiopian, Indian, and Brazilian combos sell three $0.95–1.40 batch items at $18–22 — three high-margin dishes in one ticket.
3. **Fast-service drives lunch volume.** Pad thai, banh mi, gazpacho, hummus, falafel, bibimbap, mac & cheese — under 8 min from prep. Your $13–17 lunch tier.
4. **Use 🥘 to plan prep.** Anything tagged 🥘 belongs on a Mon/Wed/Fri rotating bulk-prep schedule.
5. **Two-tier where the faux-meat alt is meaningful.** "Bibimbap $15 / with Chunk Steak $22" — a +$5–9 upsell with no extra labor.
6. **Quote faux-meat quarterly.** Impossible and Beyond have cut foodservice prices ~15–20% twice since 2021; the category is still volatile.
